entries.name: hello-world
entries.description: A tiny test skill. Writes a greeting into ~/.claude/skills/.
entries.author: Launchpad Editorial
entries.author_credential: Seed skill maintained by the Launchpad editorial team.
entries.who_its_for: Anyone verifying a fresh Launchpad install from end to end.

integrations.note: Writes a greeting file into ~/.claude/skills/.

reviews.body: Exactly what you want in an onboarding skill. No surprises, install grammar reads in 20 seconds.
Use this as a teaching example with every new team member we onboard to Claude Code.

entries.readme_md:
## What this skill does

`hello-world` is Launchpad's smallest end-to-end example — the skill equivalent of `print('hello')`. It ships one file (`SKILL.md`) and one pair of install commands that copy that file into `~/.claude/skills/hello-world/`.

It exists for two reasons:

1. **Smoke testing** the full run / install / verify flow without depending on any external repo.
2. **Teaching** the shape of a Reviewed-tier skill. The install commands use only closed-grammar verbs (`mkdir`, `cp`), so the pattern-analyzer + PR validator both accept it cleanly.

## What it installs

After `skillz install hello-world`, you'll have:

```
~/.claude/skills/hello-world/
├── SKILL.md         # a tiny prompt that Claude Code loads
└── .skillz-lock.json # generated by the installer; tracks the SHA
```

## Reading it as a template

Clone this and rename it as a starting point for your own skill:

```bash
skillz init my-first-skill
```

`skillz init` produces a Reviewed-eligible scaffold by default — no shell, no network, just files. That's the path of least resistance to a green **Reviewed** badge in the gallery.

## Verifying before install

Every skill's SHA is pinned in the registry. When you run:

```bash
skillz run hello-world
```

the CLI fetches the archive at that exact commit, shows you a two-panel diff of the files + install commands, and only executes when you type `yes`. Source is always one click away — `github.com/nolanwang-uk/hello-world` — so you can read the whole thing in less than a minute.

entries.name: sec-10k-reviewer
entries.description: Reviews a public-company 10-K against historical SEC comment-letter patterns and flags items most likely to draw staff correspondence.
entries.author: Mara Ellison
entries.author_credential: Former SEC Division of Corporation Finance staff attorney (2014–2021). Demo entry.
entries.who_its_for: Disclosure counsel at public companies preparing or reviewing annual reports before filing.

integrations.note: Reads the 10-K draft from disk.
Cross-references historical SEC comment letters.
Writes a flagged-items sheet for disclosure counsel review.

reviews.body: Flagged two risk-factor phrasings that got us a comment letter last year. Worth the price on the first review.
Strong on Item 7 MD&A. Weaker on executive compensation disclosures — still a useful first pass.

entries.name: patent-prior-art-scanner
entries.description: Structures a patent application's independent claims and drafts prior-art search queries tuned to each claim's technical vocabulary.
entries.author: Mara Ellison
entries.author_credential: USPTO-registered patent agent (Reg. No. redacted). 7 years in software prior-art. Demo entry.
entries.who_its_for: Patent agents and attorneys preparing prior-art searches before filing a non-provisional.

integrations.note: USPTO patent full-text and image database (PatFT).
Drops the claim-mapped query set into the matter's Notion page.

reviews.body: Claim-parser output is solid; the search queries need a human tune-up but that's expected.

entries.name: icd10-chart-auditor
entries.description: Audits outpatient encounter notes against ICD-10-CM coding guidelines and surfaces undersupported primary-diagnosis selections.
entries.author: Dr. Priya Natarajan
entries.author_credential: AHIMA-credentialed CCS, 12 years in ambulatory coding compliance. Demo entry.
entries.who_its_for: Coding auditors and CDI specialists reviewing encounters before claim submission.

integrations.note: Reads encounter-note export (HL7 / PDF / text).
Routes patient-chart reads through the buyer's Epic MCP server. PHI stays on the buyer's infra.
Builds an audit workbook flagging undersupported primary-dx rows.

reviews.body: Caught three underspecified primary-dx selections in the first ten charts we ran through it. Paid for itself on day one.
The explanations cite the exact guideline section. That's what sold my medical director.

entries.name: clinical-trial-protocol-reader
entries.description: Reads a clinical trial protocol and extracts primary endpoint, secondary endpoints, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and statistical power assumptions.
entries.author: Dr. Priya Natarajan
entries.author_credential: Biostatistician, 9 years in Phase II/III oncology trials. Demo entry.
entries.who_its_for: Clinical research associates and trial-design reviewers orienting themselves to a new protocol.

integrations.note: Ingests the protocol PDF.
Pulls cited references for sanity-check.
Writes a structured summary page per trial into the CRO's Notion workspace.

reviews.body: Onboarding a new protocol used to take me a full afternoon. This gets me 80% of the way there in ten minutes.

entries.name: saas-arr-bridge
entries.description: Walks a SaaS ARR bridge (new, expansion, contraction, churn) and reconciles the waterfall against the underlying subscription ledger.
entries.author: Jordan Avery
entries.author_credential: FP&A lead at two Series-C SaaS companies. CPA (inactive). Demo entry.
entries.who_its_for: SaaS FP&A and finance operators closing the month and explaining the ARR number to the board.

integrations.note: Reads the ARR bridge; writes the reconciliation deltas back as a second sheet.
Cross-references subscription ledger against the bridge.
Parameterized query against the buyer's subscription_events view.
Posts a variance summary to the finance close channel.

reviews.body: Found a $40K variance between our bridge and the subscription ledger that no one had caught for two quarters. That alone is worth the subscription.
Great foundation. We had to teach it our custom enterprise-tier accounting, but the framework held up.

entries.name: pr-description-writer
entries.description: Reads your branch diff plus linked issues and drafts a PR description with summary, motivation, test plan, and rollout notes.
entries.author: Kofi Mensah
entries.author_credential: Principal engineer at a dev-tools company. Decade of shipping open-source tooling to 500K+ developers. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Engineers who want their PRs to get reviewed in hours, not days. Writes the description in your team's voice after two sample PRs.

integrations.note: Reads the branch diff and any linked files.
GitHub + GitLab issue APIs for linked-ticket context.
Pulls acceptance criteria from linked Linear issues.

reviews.body: Cut our PR-review time in half. Reviewers actually read the description now because it tells them what to look for.
The style-match from two sample PRs is the killer feature. Worth the zero dollars and the zero dollars after that.

entries.name: release-notes-writer
entries.description: Builds versioned release notes from git history, linked issues, and semver conventions. Separates breaking changes, features, fixes, and migration steps.
entries.author: Kofi Mensah
entries.author_credential: Principal engineer at a dev-tools company. Decade of shipping open-source tooling to 500K+ developers. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Maintainers who cut releases at least monthly and want the notes to say more than 'various improvements'.

integrations.note: Reads git history; writes CHANGELOG.md + RELEASE-NOTES.md.
Pulls issue titles + labels from GitHub/GitLab to enrich the changelog.
Optional: posts the release summary to a chosen channel.

reviews.body: First changelog generator that actually understands breaking vs non-breaking. Our SEMVER is tighter since we started using this.

entries.name: flaky-test-triage
entries.description: Ingests CI logs from the last N runs, clusters flaky tests by failure signature, and proposes a quarantine or fix order based on flake rate × blast radius.
entries.author: Alexei Volkov
entries.author_credential: Staff SRE. 11 years running incident response and test-platform teams at two hyperscalers. Contributor to the SRE Workbook. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Teams whose CI is slower than it should be because the same five tests fail half the time and nobody has owned them in months.

integrations.note: Consumes exported CI logs (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Buildkite).
Optional: pulls recent workflow runs via a read-only GitHub token.
Files one ticket per flake cluster, owner-tagged by blame.
Posts a weekly 'worst flakes' digest to the platform channel.

reviews.body: Found a WebSocket-timing flake that had been failing silently for 6 months. Shipped a fix the same afternoon.
Clustering is sharp. Wished it auto-created the Jira tickets without a follow-up prompt — told the author, he said it's coming.

entries.name: otel-trace-explainer
entries.description: Reads an OpenTelemetry trace export, identifies the critical path, and writes a plain-English 'why was this slow' paragraph grounded in specific spans.
entries.author: Alexei Volkov
entries.author_credential: Staff SRE. 11 years running incident response and test-platform teams at two hyperscalers. Contributor to the SRE Workbook. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Engineers woken up at 2am to debug a P99 latency spike who don't want to learn trace-vendor UIs at 2am.

integrations.note: OTLP JSON export from your collector.
Optional: live Tempo / Jaeger query endpoint for additional context.
Optional: posts the explanation to an incident channel.

reviews.body: Finally, a tool that reads a 1400-span trace and tells me the one DB call that was the problem. Saved me 45 minutes on our last incident.

entries.name: postgres-query-optimizer
entries.description: Takes a slow query + EXPLAIN output and proposes targeted index changes, query rewrites, or plan hints. Shows expected vs actual cost math.
entries.author: Reza Haidari
entries.author_credential: Postgres DBA, 16 years. Ex-Oracle ACE. Maintains a widely-cited query-plan cheatsheet. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Backend engineers who know their app is slow but don't have a full DBA on the team — the common case.

integrations.note: Read-only connection; runs EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) with a statement timeout.
Writes suggested migration SQL to disk — never executes.

reviews.body: Turned a 4.2s query into 38ms by pointing at a missing composite index. The explanation walked me through WHY — I actually learned something.
The plan-math is correct. Rare in this space. I audit every recommendation and Reza gets it right about 9 times out of 10.

entries.name: cve-patch-advisor
entries.description: Reads a CVE advisory plus your lockfile (npm, pip, cargo, go.mod) and tells you which dependency to bump, whether the fix is backward-compatible, and what to test.
entries.author: Sam Shah
entries.author_credential: Security researcher. Ex-chair of a CNCF security SIG. Disclosed 40+ CVEs across popular OSS. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Engineers assigned to 'deal with that critical CVE' at 4:45pm on a Friday. Replaces an hour of Snyk-report-reading with a targeted diff.

integrations.note: Reads package manifest + lockfile.
NVD + GitHub Security Advisories. Allowlisted hosts only.

reviews.body: Told us a CVE everyone was panicking about didn't actually affect our version path. Saved 2 eng-days of speculative patching.
The compatibility-risk scoring is the best I've seen. Sam clearly reads the commit diffs, not just the advisories.

entries.name: mcp-server-auditor
entries.description: Audits an MCP server configuration for over-scoped tools, unvalidated inputs, and implicit secrets in returned data. Produces a risk-ranked report.
entries.author: Sam Shah
entries.author_credential: Security researcher. Ex-chair of a CNCF security SIG. Disclosed 40+ CVEs across popular OSS. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Platform teams spinning up MCP servers for their org who want the audit done before the first skill talks to it.

integrations.note: Inspects the server's tool manifest + test invocations.
Writes the audit report as markdown + JSON.

reviews.body: Caught a tool in our internal MCP that was returning raw database rows with a created_by field containing user emails. Would have been a mess.

entries.name: dbt-model-review
entries.description: Reviews a dbt model PR against the project's graph, flagging fan-out joins, missing tests, late-binding references, and silent schema drift.
entries.author: Nadia Khoury
entries.author_credential: Analytics engineer, 8 years. Former dbt Labs field engineering team. Maintainer of two popular dbt packages. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Analytics teams whose dbt repo is big enough that no one person holds the full DAG in their head.

integrations.note: Reads the dbt project + manifest.json.
Optional: live schema introspection for drift checks.
Optional: live schema introspection for drift checks.

reviews.body: Caught a join that would have 5x'd our warehouse spend if it merged. On a Monday. We now run this on every dbt PR.

entries.name: employment-offer-letter-review
entries.description: Reviews a US tech employment offer letter for practitioner-favorable language: clawbacks, double-trigger acceleration, non-solicit scope, PTO treatment, arbitration clauses.
entries.author: Hana Voss
entries.author_credential: Employment attorney, 15 years advising tech employees on offers + departures. California + New York bars. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Engineers and designers weighing an offer this week who aren't going to hire a lawyer but should. Reviews one PDF, returns a two-page negotiation brief.

integrations.note: Reads the offer PDF or DOCX.

reviews.body: Flagged a single-trigger clawback I would have signed. Negotiated it out in the next counteroffer.
The negotiation brief is what you actually need — not a 40-page legal memo. This is the ideal format for this kind of work.

entries.name: independent-1099-reconciler
entries.description: Reconciles a US freelancer's 1099-NEC and 1099-K forms against their invoicing ledger and bank deposits. Flags mismatches, missing forms, and double-reported income.
entries.author: Ricardo Alves
entries.author_credential: CPA, 12 years. Focus on independent-contractor tax for software and creative professionals. MVP prototype entry.
entries.who_its_for: Freelancers and small LLCs whose 1099s never quite match what they actually earned — which is most of them.

integrations.note: Reads 1099 PDFs, invoice CSVs, bank statement exports.
Optional: pulls invoicing data directly from QuickBooks Online.
Writes a reconciliation workbook with a tab per 1099.

reviews.body: Found a 1099-K from a platform I hadn't noticed that double-reported income I'd already logged. Saved me an eight-hour CPA bill.
